pr.title: chore(lint): pyproject.toml as single source of truth for ruff
pr.body:
## Summary

- Move the full ruff config into `[tool.ruff]` in `pyproject.toml` — the Python-ecosystem standard location.
- Drop the explicit `--config dev_config/python/ruff.toml` flags from the pre-commit hooks so ruff auto-discovers `pyproject.toml`.
- Delete the now-redundant `dev_config/python/ruff.toml`.
- Bump pre-commit ruff from `v0.11.8` → `v0.12.5` to match the pinned dev dependency.

## Why

Two configs were silently diverging:

| | `dev_config/python/ruff.toml` (CI) | `[tool.ruff]` in `pyproject.toml` (local) |
|---|---|---|
| Quote style | single ✅ | not enforced ❌ |
| Q / B / ASYNC / UP rules | ✅ | ❌ |
| D (docstring) rules | ❌ | ✅ |

Code that passed `uv run ruff` locally could still fail CI with hundreds of fixable errors (311 quote-style fixes landed as a follow-up commit in #14295).

## Excludes and ignores — why they're needed

### `exclude: ^(enterprise/)` on the pre-commit hooks
Was already present in the old `ruff.toml`. Enterprise has its own separate `Lint enterprise python` CI job with different rules. Without this pre-commit-level exclude, ruff would run on enterprise files with the OSS config in addition to the enterprise config.

### `lint.ignore` entries in `pyproject.toml`
All but one were copied verbatim from the old `ruff.toml`:

| Rule | Reason |
|------|---------|
| `E501` | Line length — enforcing would touch hundreds of files |
| `B003, B007, B009, B010, B018, B904` | Pre-existing bugbear violations across the repo |
| `ASYNC110, 220, 221, 230, 251` | Already ignored before; comment says "fix in a separate PR" |
| `ASYNC109` | **New here** — rule didn't exist in ruff 0.11.8 (old CI version); pre-existing violations in `app_server/` on main that were never caught. Ignoring preserves the status quo rather than requiring a repo-wide fix in this PR. |

### D rules not included in `lint.select`
The old CI config never enforced D (docstring) rules — only the old local `pyproject.toml` did. Adding them now would surface hundreds of pre-existing violations across the repo and block CI. They can be re-added in a dedicated clean-up PR once the backlog is cleared.

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `pyproject.toml` | Full ruff config in dotted-key format (pyproject-fmt-stable) |
| `dev_config/python/.pre-commit-config.yaml` | Remove `--config` flags; add `exclude: ^(enterprise/)` back; bump ruff to `v0.12.5` |
| `dev_config/python/ruff.toml` | Deleted |
| `openhands/app_server/app_lifespan/alembic/versions/*.py` | Auto-fix pre-existing `UP007`/`F401` violations (ruff was modifying these in CI, causing hook failures) |
| `openhands/app_server/sandbox/remote_sandbox_service.py` | Same — auto-fix `UP007` |

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
pr.author: simonrosenberg
